title: THE naming break: fillet an edge, then edit an upstream sketch dimension, regen
source: OneCAD-CPP/TODO.md:150-154 — 'H5-B PARKED'. Verbatim root-cause of the topological-naming failure and the redesign direction. Repro test 'testFilletSurvivesUpstreamCutEdit' lives ONLY in reverted commit 98b8083 (wiring attempt reverted in bae765e) — it is NOT in the frozen tree, so it cannot be run.
OneCAD-CPP/HANDOFF.md:29,41,45,72 — H5 A-half shipped (loud failure over wrong-face); H5-B redesign (parametric anchors) is future/unscheduled; tombstones+adoption alone insufficient.

OneCAD-CPP/src/app/history/RegenerationEngine.cpp + ElementMap.h:304-320 removeElementsForBody — the pre-reset erasure: regen erases every body element entry before replay, so descriptor-hash ids only reproduce for IDENTICAL geometry.

OneCAD-CPP/src/kernel/elementmap/ElementMap.h:762-787 makeChildId + :703-760 makeKey/stableHash — ids are deterministic FNV-1a hashes of the quantized (1e-6) descriptor => any geometry change from a parameter edit changes the hash => downstream edge/face references permanently orphan.
OneCAD-CPP/src/kernel/elementmap/ElementMap.h:212-264 resolveWithFallback — cannot recover an ERASED entry (no stored descriptor to re-match).

OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_regeneration.cpp:1310-1338 — 'Re-profiling an extrude swaps its source region' (500->180->500). Shows a parametric edit that DOES regen cleanly TODAY — precisely because the extrude has NO downstream op referencing its geometry ids. Contrast anchor for the break.
protocol/SCHEMA.md §9 (NeedsRepair payload), §10 (resolution ladder), §11 (Invariants 1,2,3) — the NEW required behavior.

entities: rectangle 10 x 10 (a Distance constraint drives the width that will be edited)

opScript.note: Produces body_1, a 10x10x5 box. Volume 500 before edit (proto_regeneration.cpp:1325 analog).

intent.descriptor: frozen descriptor of the top-rim edge at z=5

opScript.note: Fillet references a body_1 edge by ElementMap id (old scheme) / semanticRef (new scheme).
Upstream sketch DIMENSION edit (width 10 -> 14). Then full regen replays sketch -> extrude -> fillet.

oldStack_behaviorToday.outcome: BROKEN — orphaned reference (H5-B). Regen erases body_1's element entries before replay (removeElementsForBody); the re-extruded box has a NEW top-rim edge whose descriptor-hash id differs from the frozen fillet edgeId (geometry changed => hash changed). The fillet's edgeIds no longer resolve; resolveWithFallback cannot recover an erased entry (no stored descriptor).
symptoms: Fillet edge reference orphaned (silently dropped or op fails).
Descriptor drift: following the fillet's own Modified chain drags the input edge id onto the blend boundary (z=4 -> z=3 in the repro), corrupting identity for the next replay (TODO.md:152).
Tombstone variant: sibling blend-boundary tombstones outscore the true orphan for the recreated rim edge (TODO.md:153).
oldStack_behaviorToday.provenance: OneCAD-CPP/TODO.md:150-154 (parked). NOT reproducible at the frozen commit (repro test is in reverted 98b8083). Recorded as the ANTI-GOAL.

newStack_requiredOutcome.primary: OCCT-history rebind: the worker keeps builder objects alive and consults Modified/Generated of ALL ops in the step (extrude + fillet), so the fillet's edge follows the re-extruded rim and the fillet SURVIVES the width edit. A unique history image auto-binds (SCHEMA §10 ladder step 1).
newStack_requiredOutcome.fallback: If history is ambiguous/absent, descriptor+anchor matching narrows; if still not a confident unique match => NeedsRepair STATE (SCHEMA §9), NEVER a silent wrong bind.
hardInvariants: Invariant 1: the fillet edge's ElementId never changes merely because geometry changed (SCHEMA §11).
Invariant 2: the frozen descriptor is EVIDENCE, never identity — it does not become the id.
Invariant 3: the fillet resolves its input on the EXACT predecessor snapshot (the re-extruded box), never overwriting the stored anchor with the fillet's own output (the z=4->z=3 drift bug).
newStack_requiredOutcome.failureContract: A NeedsRepair at the fillet step still prepares snapshot m-1 and returns a successful PlanPrepared (stoppedReason 'needsRepair'); it is STATE, not an Err (SCHEMA §8).

controlCase_noDownstreamRef.scenario: Same width edit on an extrude with NO downstream topology reference (the re-profile test).
controlCase_noDownstreamRef.meaning: Confirms the break is specifically about downstream references to descriptor-hash ids, not about parametric regen in general.

notes: This is the single unsolved defect the migration exists to fix. The corpus records the OLD outcome (orphan) as the anti-goal and the NEW outcome (history rebind, else NeedsRepair) as the golden. The new stack's calibration must prove: fillet-survives-upstream-edit when history is unambiguous, and deterministic NeedsRepair (never wrong bind) when it is not. See case_f for the deliberately-ambiguous companion.
